publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

sourceDesc.p: Work of the literatures of Ethiopia and Eritrea
A TEI file converted from the ETHIO-authority google spreadsheet

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

assertion.unit: First Chapter: On the love of God, humility, charity, chastity, solitude, etc.

t:abstract.t:p: The 35 chapters on the monastic life known in Gǝʿǝz as Maṣḥafa Mār Yǝṣhāq have been translated from Arabic. The Arabic version has been translated by Abū l-Fatḥ ʿAbdallāh b. al-Faḍl from Greek and consitutes the third volume of Isaac of Nineveh's Arabic works. Some of the chapters are preserved in the original Syriac.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

relation.name: saws:isAttributedToAuthor
relation.t:desc: Some of the texts in are originally written by Isaac of Nineveh.

relation.name: saws:isAttributedToAuthor
relation.t:desc: Abū l-Fatḥ ʿAbdallāh b. al-Faḍl translated the 35 chapters on the monastic life from Greek to Arabic, adding a preface addressed to Isaac of Nineveh and stating the name of his patron, Nicephorus al-Qabqalis. Geschichte der christlichen arabischen Literatur. Zweiter Band: Die Schriftsteller bis zur Mitte des 15. JahrhundertsStudi e TestiGeorgGrafCittà del VaticanoBiblioteca Apostolica Vaticana1947Studi e Testi133II133bm:Graf1947GCALIIbm:Graf1947GCALII58

relation.name: saws:isAttributedToAuthor
relation.t:desc: This discourse is attributed to Isaac of Nineveh, but is part of the ascetic homilies of John Saba.

t:bibl.title: Isaac of Nineveh
EAe
Encyclopaedia Aethiopica
t:bibl.publisher: Harrassowitz Verlag

t:bibl.title: Das Mashafa Mar Yeshaq von Ninive. Einleitung, Edition und Übersetzung mit Kommentar
t:bibl.publisher: Kovač

t:div.t:label: Preface by the translator Abū l-Fatḥ ʿAbdallāh b. al-Faḍl
First Chapter: On the love of God, humility, charity, chastity, solitude, etc.

t:bibl.title: Bibliothecae apostolicae vaticanae codicum manuscriptorum catalogus in tres partes distributus in quarum prima orientales in altera graeci in tertia latini italici aliorumque europaeorum idiomatum codices
t:bibl.publisher: Ex typographia linguarum orientalium apud Komarek ad Ortorium Sancti Marcelli

t:bibl.title: Eusebii Alexandrini Episcopi, Eusebii Emeseni, Leontii Byzantini opera quae reperiri potuerunt omnia; intermiscentur Theodori Scythopolitani, Theodosii Alexandrini, S. Gregentii Tapharensis, Epiphanii Cp., S. Isaaci Ninivitani, Episcorum; Timothei Cp., Timothei Hierosolymitani, Presbyterorum; Joannis Maxentii, Theodori Lectoris, Procopii Presbyteri Tyrorum, S. Barsanuphii, Anachoreatae Palaestini, Agapeti Cp. Diaconi, Eustathii Monachi, Epistolae, Tractatus, Opuscula; accedunt Justiniani, Imperatoris Augustis, Scripta Dogmatica

Patrologiae Cursus Completus
t:bibl.publisher: Excudebatur et venit apud J.-P. Migne editorem

t:bibl.title: Bibliotheca Orientalis Clementino-Vaticana: In Qua Manuscriptos Codices Syriacos, Arabicos, Persicos, Turcicos, Hebraicos, Samaritanos, Armenicos, Aethiopicos, Graecos, Aegyptiacos, Ibericos, & Malabaricos, Jussu Et Munificentia Clementis XI. Pontificis Maximi Ex Oriente conquisitos, comparatos, avectos, & Bibliothecae Vaticanae addictos
t:bibl.publisher: Typis Sacræ Congregationis de Propaganda Fide

t:bibl.title: Eusebii Alexandrini Episcopi, Eusebii Emeseni, Leontii Byzantini opera quae reperiri potuerunt omnia; intermiscentur Theodori Scythopolitani, Theodosii Alexandrini, S.
